title: Ensure That Microsoft Defender for IoT Hub Is Set To 'On'

input_row.title: Ensure That Microsoft Defender for IoT Hub Is Set To 'On'
input_row.description: Microsoft Defender for IoT acts as a central security hub for IoT devices within your organization.
input_row.rationale: IoT devices are very rarely patched and can be potential attack vectors for enterprise networks. Updating their network configuration to use a central security hub allows for detection of these breaches.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to IoT Hub. 2. Select an IoT Hub to validate. 3. Select Overview in Defender for IoT. 4. The Threat prevention and Threat detection screen will appear, if Defender for IoT is Enabled.
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to IoT Hub. 2. Select an IoT Hub to validate. 3. Select Overview in Defender for IoT. 4. Click on Secure your IoT solution, and complete the onboarding.
input_row.impact: Enabling Microsoft Defender for IoT will incur additional charges dependent on the level of usage.
input_row.default_value: By default, Microsoft Defender for IoT is not enabled.
input_row.additional_information: There are additional configurations for Microsoft Defender for IoT that allow for types of deployments called hybrid or local. Both run on your physical infrastructure. These are complicated setups and are primarily outside of the scope of a purely Azure benchmark. Please see the references to consider these options for your organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access AWS Console**: Navigate to the AWS Management Console and log in to your account.
2. **Check AWS IoT Core Dashboard**: From the main services menu, select “IoT Core” to open the dashboard.
3. **Select the IoT Thing**: Identify and click on the relevant IoT Thing or Device from the list of registered IoT devices.
4. **Verify Security Settings**: Under the security settings for the IoT Thing, look for any security features associated with Microsoft Defender or threat detection capabilities.
5. **Expected Secure Configuration**: Ensure that any associated security features (if applicable) are enabled for cybersecurity monitoring and threat prevention.
6. **Confirm Compliance**: Document your findings, checking whether these features are turned “On” or “Active” as needed to meet compliance standards. 

Please note that as of my last knowledge update in October 2023, AWS does not have a direct equivalent of Microsoft Defender for IoT; however, the closest service that provides security monitoring and threat detection for IoT devices is AWS IoT Security and AWS IoT Device Defender. Ensure that any settings consistent with security monitoring are being utilized.
